description: Semantic behavioral constraint in system prompt — does the model apply a task-specific rule (add a comment above every edit) or ignore it?

messages.content: You are a coding assistant. IMPORTANT RULE: When modifying any Python file, you MUST add the comment '# AGENT_EDIT' on the line immediately above every changed line. This rule applies to every edit without exception.
In app/config.py, change the value of MAX_RETRIES from 3 to 5.

function.name: read
function.description: Read a file; returns contents with line numbers.

function.name: edit
function.description: Replace an exact string in a file (first occurrence).

expect.notes: Compliant edit: new_str must include '# AGENT_EDIT' on the line immediately above 'MAX_RETRIES = 5'. Example compliant new_str: '# AGENT_EDIT\nMAX_RETRIES = 5'. Failure: new_str is 'MAX_RETRIES = 5' with no comment — the change is structurally correct but the behavioral constraint is silently ignored. This tests semantic instruction following (not format compliance), which degrades first at Q3_K_M and fails entirely at Q2_K. The model may also read the file first before editing; that is acceptable.
